# webauthn-server-buildkit — full reference for language models

> A framework-independent WebAuthn / FIDO2 / passkeys SERVER library for
> TypeScript and Node.js. npm: webauthn-server-buildkit. License: MIT.
> Author: Ahsan Mahmood (https://aoneahsan.com). Current version: 2.3.0.
> Node.js >= 24.13.0. One runtime dependency: cbor-x.

This file expands llms.txt with the concrete public API so an LLM can answer
"how do I do X with webauthn-server-buildkit" accurately. Everything below
reflects the real, shipping API.

## Install & import

    npm install webauthn-server-buildkit
    import { WebAuthnServer, MemoryStorageAdapter } from 'webauthn-server-buildkit';

## Construction

    new WebAuthnServer({
      rpName, rpID, origin, encryptionSecret,   // required; encryptionSecret >= 32 chars
      storageAdapter?, sessionDuration?, attestationType?,
      enableMobileAttestation?, enforceChallengeStore?, allowCrossOriginIframe?,
      originVerifier?, userVerification?, authenticatorSelection?, supportedAlgorithms?,
      challengeSize?, timeout?, preferredAuthenticatorType?, metadataService?,
      requireTrustedAttestation?, debug?, logger?,
    })

Defaults: attestationType 'none', enforceChallengeStore true, allowCrossOriginIframe
false, requireTrustedAttestation false, userVerification 'preferred',
supportedAlgorithms [ES256, RS256], challengeSize 32, timeout 60000,
sessionDuration 86400000 (24h), enableMobileAttestation false.

## WebAuthnServer methods

- createRegistrationOptions(user, params?) -> { options, challenge, webAuthnUserId }
- verifyRegistration(response, expectedChallenge, expectedOrigin?, { existingCredentialIds? }?) -> { verified, registrationInfo? }
- createAuthenticationOptions(params?) -> { options, challenge }
- verifyAuthentication(response, expectedChallenge, credential, expectedOrigin?) -> { verified, authenticationInfo? }
- createSession(userId, credentialId, userVerified, additionalData?) -> string (encrypted token)
- validateSession(token) -> { valid, sessionData?, sessionId? }
- refreshSession(token) -> string
- revokeSession(token) -> void
- revokeUserSessions(userId) -> void
- cleanup() -> void
- getStorageAdapter() -> StorageAdapter | undefined

## Attestation

Formats verified: none, packed (self + x5c), fido-u2f, android-key, tpm,
android-safetynet, apple. attestationVerified is true ONLY when trust-anchored —
apple via the bundled APPLE_WEBAUTHN_ROOT_CA_PEM, the chain formats via a
metadataService (FIDO MDS). Use StaticMetadataService({ defaultRoots: [...] }).
Set requireTrustedAttestation: true to reject non-trust-anchored attestation.
No CRL/OCSP (offline by design). android-safetynet is deprecated (prefer Play
Integrity). Exports: StaticMetadataService, APPLE_WEBAUTHN_ROOT_CA_PEM,
verifyAppleAttestation/verifyPackedAttestation/verifyFidoU2fAttestation/
verifyAndroidKeyAttestation/verifyTpmAttestation/verifyAndroidSafetyNetAttestation,
plus X.509 helpers (parseCert, verifyCertChain, publicKeyMatchesCOSE, ...) and a
minimal DER reader.

## Storage adapter

Interface with four sections — users, credentials, challenges, sessions — each a
set of async methods. MemoryStorageAdapter ships for dev/tests. find* methods
return null when absent. publicKey is a Uint8Array.

## Errors

All errors extend WebAuthnError with a stable open-union `code` and a `statusCode`
(400 input/verification, 401 auth/session, 500 config/storage). Subclasses:
RegistrationError, AuthenticationError, VerificationError, ConfigurationError,
StorageError, SessionError. Branch on `code`, not the message.

## Honest scope

Server-only (no browser/native client). Requires a secure context (HTTPS or
localhost). enableMobileAttestation (default off) is a non-standard JSON path that
does NOT do cryptographic WebAuthn attestation — use only with full client/
transport control.

## Key URLs

- Docs: https://webauthn-server-buildkit-docs.aoneahsan.com
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/webauthn-server-buildkit
- Source: https://github.com/aoneahsan/webauthn-server-buildkit
